basics.name: Eric Bartos
basics.label: Senior Infrastructure Architect
basics.summary: Senior Infrastructure Architect with 8+ years designing and managing enterprise infrastructure supporting 300+ users across 7 locations. Expert in VMware virtualization (29 hosts, 350+ VMs in production), Palo Alto security (18 devices in production), and multi-site network architecture. Proven track record managing $8M+ technology portfolios with 99.9%+ uptime and leading $4M+ infrastructure projects. Experienced in remote infrastructure management and distributed team collaboration.

work.name: Wisconsin Public Radio

positions.title: Senior Infrastructure Architect (Broadcast Network Integration Engineer)

highlights: Lead enterprise infrastructure architecture supporting 300+ employees across 7 locations with $8M+ technology portfolio and 99.9%+ uptime
Architect and manage 29-host VMware environment with 350+ VMs across primary, DR, and remote sites including HA/DRS clustering, vMotion, and VM lifecycle management
Design and maintain enterprise security architecture with 18 Palo Alto firewalls in 9 HA pairs, centralized Panorama management, IDS/IPS, and zero-trust network segmentation
Implement multi-site network with 100G backbone connectivity, EVPN/VXLAN overlay on Cisco Nexus/NDFC, BGP/OSPF routing, and site-to-site VPN
Manage enterprise storage infrastructure including 300TB+ capacity across Dell PowerStore/Compellent and IXSystems TrueNAS (iSCSI/NFS)
Operate monitoring and observability stack with Elastic Stack and Zenoss covering SNMP, syslog, and network alerting
Manage PKI/TLS certificate infrastructure, DNS/DHCP services, and firewall policy across all locations
Support broadcast network infrastructure including Audio over IP (Dante/AES67) and studio systems

work.name: Nexcess.net
highlights: Contributed to physical buildout of new multi-hall datacenter facility across two summers, laying infrastructure before facility opened
Led structured cabling, optical fiber runs, transceiver installation, PDU configuration, and MDF/IDF physical plant preparation
Performed rack and stack, server builds, Linux provisioning, and pre-configuration of production systems

work.name: Michigan Technological University

positions.title: Technical Operations Manager — WMTU 91.9 FM & Student Telecom

highlights: Led campus radio station broadcast infrastructure design and technical operations management
Implemented Audio over IP networking solutions and enterprise telecommunications systems
Managed technical staff and training programs

projects.name: Network Architecture Overhaul
projects.description: Designed and executed migration from legacy switching to Cisco Nexus with NDFC fabric controller and EVPN/VXLAN overlay architecture. Implemented 100G backbone connectivity, BGP/OSPF routing, VRFs with route leaking, and network segmentation across all sites.

projects.name: DR Site Design & Buildout
projects.description: Leading complete disaster recovery facility design and buildout including compute, storage, and network replication. Encompasses VMware site recovery, storage replication, and network path redundancy to ensure business continuity.

projects.name: Colocated Row Build & Commissioning
projects.description: Designed and commissioned a colocated datacenter row including power distribution, structured cabling, optical interconnects, and rack infrastructure for production workloads.

projects.name: Enterprise Storage Migration
projects.description: Led migration from Dell PowerStore/Compellent to IXSystems TrueNAS with zero downtime for 300+ users. Migrated 300TB+ of iSCSI and NFS workloads while maintaining full production availability.

projects.name: VMware Infrastructure Modernization
projects.description: Executed 12-host hardware refresh and vSphere upgrade across the production environment. Included server builds, ESXi deployment, vCenter reconfiguration, and VM migration with HA/DRS optimization.

projects.name: Complete Datacenter Buildout
projects.description: Led initial enterprise datacenter design, physical layout, and full implementation including power distribution, structured cabling, MDF/IDF design, and optical networking infrastructure.

courses: Transferred to Michigan Technological University, Spring 2013
